observations.summary: Dan finds his father's old walking stick by the hearth, described as made of ash wood, fire-hardened at the tip, and smooth from years of use.

evidence.quote: By the hearth, where the last coals still glowed a dull, sullen red, Dan found his father's old walking stick. Ash wood, fire-hardened at the tip, smooth from years of use.

observations.notes: The walking stick symbolizes a connection to the father and his past.

observations.summary: Aviel, Dan's father, carried the walking stick when he was a scholar and used to walk to houses of learning where men sought his counsel.

evidence.quote: Aviel had carried it when he was still a scholar, back when he'd walk to the houses of learning, when men sought his counsel.

observations.notes: This indicates Aviel's former respected status and intellectual role.
